title: Method for the extraction of palm kernel oil (pko) and coconut oil (co) from various palm kernel sources and copra utilizing supercritical carbon dioxide (sc-co2) to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre
abstract: This patent details a method for the extraction of palm kernel oil (PKO) and defatted palm kernel fibre from various sources of palm kernel. Three sources of palm kernel are outlined: raw palm kernel with testa, palm kernel without testa and palm kernel cake (PKC). This patent also details a method for the extraction of coconut oil (CO) from copra. Supercritical carbon dioxide (SC-CO 2 ) is used as a solvent in the extraction process to extract the PKO and CO from the palm kernel and copra matrix. Parameters governing the SC-CO 2 extraction process, namely; temperature, pressure, SC-CO 2 flow rate and palm kernel particle size, are set at levels which allow the maximum amounts of PKO and CO to be extracted from the palm kernel and copra matrix. Once the extraction process is completed, two products are obtained from palm kernel: palm kernel oil and defatted palm kernel fibre. In the case of copra, two products are also obtained namely virgin coconut oil (VCO) and defatted coconut flesh fibre. Removal of the testa from palm kernel before the oil extraction step results in the extraction of colourless oil. The patent also details the testa removal process form palm kernel.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A method for the extraction of coconut oil from copra, or for the extraction of palm kernel oil from palm kernels or palm kernel cakes, comprising: a.) removing the oils completely from the matrix of the copra, or the matrix of the palm kernel, or the matrix of the palm kernel cake. b. ) the reduction in size of the copra or palm kernels using physical means such as grinding, milling, pulverising etc. c. ) extracting the coconut oil from copra, or palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix us d. ) the extraction temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of obtaining palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix, or coconut oil from copra, using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent. e.) the extraction pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of obtaining palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix, or coconut oil from copra, using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent. f. ) the flow rate of the of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of obtaining palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix, or coconut oil from copra, using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent. g. ) the reduction of the size of the palm kernels or copra using physical means such as grinding, milling, sieving, etc., for the purpose of obtaining palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix, or coconut oil from copra, using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent. 2. A method for the extraction of palm kernel oil from palm kernels or palm kernel cakes for the purpose of obtaining defatted palm kernel fibre which involves: a.) the reduction in size of the palm kernels using physical means such as grinding, milling, pulverising etc. b.) the use of supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent to extract the palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre. 

 c. ) the extraction temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of extracting palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre. d. ) the extraction pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of extracting palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre. e. ) the flow rate of the of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of extracting palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre. f. ) the reduction of the size of the palm kernels using physical means such as grinding, milling, sieving, etc., for the purpose of extracting palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre. g. ) The subsequent application of the defatted palm kernel fibre as a food product in itself, or as a food additive, or as flour or for subsequent incorporation into flour products. 3. A method for the extraction of coconut oil from copra for the purpose of obtaining defatted coconut flesh fibre which involves: a. ) the reduction in size of the copra using physical means such as grinding, milling, pulverising etc. b. ) the use of supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent to extract the coconut oil from the copra matrix to obtain defatted cocnut flesh fibre. c. ) the extraction temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of extracting coconut oil from the copra matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, to obtain defatted coconut flesh fibre. d. ) the extraction pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of extracting coconut oil 

 from the copra matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, to obtain defatted coconut flesh fibre. e. ) the flow rate of the of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of extracting coconut oil from the copra matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, to obtain defatted coconut flesh fibre. f. ) the reduction of the size of the palm kernels using physical means such as grinding, milling, sieving, etc., for the purpose of extracting coconut oil from the copra matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, to obtain defatted coconut flesh fibre. g. ) The subsequent application of the coconut flesh fibre as a food product in itself, or as a food additive, or as flour or for subsequent incorporation into flour products. 4. A method for the extraction of palm kernel oil from palm kernels which involves: a.) the reduction in size of the palm kernels using physical means such as grinding, milling, pulverising etc., which is carried out after the removal of the palm kernel testa from the palm kernels via physical and chemical means, b.) the use of supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent to extract the palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix. c.) the extraction temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of obtaining palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent. d. ) the extraction pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of obtaining palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent. e. ) the flow rate of the of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of obtaining palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent. f.) the reduction of the size of the palm kernels using physical means such as grinding, milling, sieving, etc., which is carried out after the removal of the palm kernel testa from the palm kernels via physical and chemical means, for the purpose of 

 obtaining palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent. 5. A method for the extraction of colourless palm kernel oil from palm kernels which involves: a. ) the removal of the palm kernel testa from the palm kernels via physical and chemical means before the extraction of the colourless palm kernel oil. b. ) the reduction in size of the palm kernels using physical means such as grinding, milling, pulverising etc., which is carried out after the removal of the palm kernel testa from the palm kernels via physical and chemical means. c. ) the use of supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent to extract the palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix. d. ) the extraction temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of obtaining palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent. e. ) the extraction pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of obtaining palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent. f. ) the flow rate of the of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of obtaining palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent. g. ) the reduction of the size of the palm kernels using physical means such as grinding, milling, sieving, etc., which is carried out after the removal of the palm kernel testa from the palm kernels via physical and chemical means, for the purpose of obtaining palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent. 6. A method for the extraction of palm kernel oil from palm kernel for the purpose of obtaining defatted palm kernel fibre which involves: a.) the removal of the palm kernel testa from the palm kernels via physical and chemical means before the extraction of the palm kernel oil. 

 b. ) the reduction in size of the palm kernels using physical means such as grinding, milling, pulverising etc., which is carried out after the removal of the palm kernel testa via physical and chemical means. c. ) the use of supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent to extract the palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre. d. ) the extraction temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of extracting palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre. e.) the extraction pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of extracting palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre. f. ) the flow rate of the of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of extracting palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre. g. ) the reduction in size of the palm kernels using physical means such as grinding, milling, pulverising sieving, etc., which is carried out after the removal of the palm kernel testa from the palm kernels via physical and chemical means, for the purpose of extracting palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre. h. ) The subsequent application of the defatted palm kernel fibre as a food product in itself, or as a food additive, or as flour or for subsequent incorporation into flour products. 7. A method for the extraction of palm kernel oil from palm kernel cake which involves: a. ) the palm kernel cake being a result of palm kernels undergoing a physical process to extract the palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix, which in turn leads to reduction of the size of the palm kernels. b. ) the use of supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent to extract the palm kernel oil from the palm kernel cake matrix. 

 c. ) the process of outlined in 5a.) occurring before the use of supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent to extract the palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix. d. ) the extraction temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of obtaining palm kernel oil from the palm kernel cake matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent. e. ) the extraction pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of obtaining palm kernel oil from the palm kernel cake matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent. f. ) the flow rate of the of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of obtaining palm kernel oil from the palm kernel cake matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent. g. ) the reduction of the size of the palm kernel cake particles using physical means such as grinding, milling, pulverising, sieving, etc., for the purpose of obtaining palm kernel oil from the palm kernel cake matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent. 8. A method for the extraction of palm kernel oil from palm kernel cake for the purpose of obtaining defatted palm kernel fibre which involves: a. ) the palm kernel cake being a result of palm kernels undergoing a physical process to extract the palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix, which in turn leads to reduction of the size of the palm kernels. b. ) the use of supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent to extract the palm kernel oil from the palm kernel cake matrix to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre. c. ) the process outlined in 6a.) occurring before the use of supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent to extract the palm kernel oil from the palm kernel matrix to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre. d. ) the extraction temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of extracting palm kernel oil from the palm kernel cake matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre. 

 e. ) the extraction pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of extracting palm kernel oil from the palm kernel cake matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre. f. ) the flow rate of the of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process being a variable parameter which is controlled for the purpose of extracting palm kernel oil from the palm kernel cake matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre. g. ) the reduction of the size of the palm kernel cake particles using physical means such as grinding, milling, pulverising, sieving, etc., for the purpose of extracting palm kernel oil from the palm kernel cake matrix using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, to obtain defatted palm kernel fibre. h. ) The subsequent application of the defatted palm kernel fibre as a food product in itself, or as a food additive, or as flour or for subsequent incorporation into flour products.
